message:
fix(sync): remove spurious baton passing when a notified waiter is dropped before poll

Notify's waiter-drop path previously checked whether the dropped
waiter held a notify_one baton (notified + matching generation)
and, if so, passed the baton to the next waiter in the queue.
This was incorrect: a waiter that was notified but never polled
to Ready should not propagate its baton on drop, because the
notification was already consumed by the generation advance.
Passing it forward caused a spurious wakeup of an unrelated
waiter, violating the notify_one contract (exactly one waiter
woken per notify_one call).

Fix: The drop path now simply removes the waiter from the slab
without attempting baton propagation.  The test
poll_a_then_notify_broadcast_then_wait_c_then_poll_both is updated
to expect waiter_c as Pending (correct behavior) instead of Ready
(spurious wakeup).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
